videos.description:
If this doesn’t get you hype for #MLPColumbus, we don’t know what will...🔥

#MLPDallas was electric, and we’re just getting started. Huge thank you to the incredible teams, fans, sponsors, vendors, special guests, and Pickler Universe for showing up and showing OUT. That’s how you kick off a season. See you back in August. 💥

#MLP2026 #MajorLeaguePickleball #pickleball

videos.title: Oceanview Named Title Sponsor of the 2026 Oceanview USA Pickleball National Championships
videos.description:
USA Pickleball is proud to announce Oceanview Life and Annuity as the new title sponsor of its premier event, now officially named the 2026 Oceanview USA Pickleball National Championships. The partnership marks a significant milestone for the organization and reinforces the continued momentum of pickleball as one of the fastest-growing sports in the country. 

Set to take place October 31–November

videos.title: What is Pickleball?
videos.description:
Pickleball is one of the fastest growing sports in the United States and for good reason. In this video, we break down what Pickleball is, why it’s so popular, and how you can get started playing.

Pickleball is a paddle sport that combines elements of tennis, table tennis, and badminton into one fun and accessible game. One of its biggest appeals is that players of all ages and skill levels can p

videos.title: Pickleball Fundamentals For Beginners
videos.description:
Having a strong foundation in pickleball fundamentals will set you up for long-term success on the court. In this video, we break down some of the most important fundamentals every beginner should learn to start playing with confidence.

First, it all starts with your grip. We recommend the continental grip, which you can find by forming a “V” with your dominant hand and placing it along the edge

videos.title: Most Important Rules to Start Playing Pickleball
videos.description:
If you don’t know the rules, you can’t play! In this video, we break down the most important pickleball rules so you can step on the court with confidence and start playing right away.

First, understand the boundaries of the court. The baselines and sidelines mark the limits, and for a ball to be in, it must land on or within these lines. If any part of the ball touches the line, it’s considered

items.title: Anna Leigh Waters' Undefeated Singles Streak by the Numbers
items.summary: Is it hyperbolic to say Anna Leigh Waters has one of the most dominant undefeated streaks in sports history? The numbers tell a convincing story.
items.author: Erik Tice

items.title: How to Defend Against a Stacking Team in Pickleball: Counter-Tactics
items.summary: Knowing how to defend against stacking in pickleball is one of the most underrated skills in doubles. This guide breaks down the counter-tactics that flip the stacking team's positioning advantage against them.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Master the Hand-Speed Drill: A 5-Step System to Dominate at the Kitchen Line
items.summary: A hand speed drill isn't just about moving your paddle faster—it's about training your entire body to react with precision at the net.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Pickleball Placement Strategy: Exactly Where to Aim Every Shot to Win More Games
items.summary: Knowing where to aim in pickleball is the difference between winning and losing. Learn the exact placement strategy for every shot from serve to attack.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Punish the Forehand Counter in Pickleball
items.summary: The forehand counter is one of the most challenging shots in pickleball, especially for continental grip players. Master this essential technique with the proven methods used by top pros.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 10 Things Advanced Pickleball Players Do Differently
items.summary: An advanced pickleball player doesn't just hit harder or faster. They think differently about the game, from footwork to strategy to mental toughness. Here are the 10 habits that separate the best from the rest.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Punish Players Who Sit Backhand at the Kitchen
items.summary: To beat players who sit backhand, stop feeding their paddle with hard, flat speedups. Use a slower, heavier roll into the shoulder or upper-body zone. That higher ball can jam their backhand counter, force awkward contact, and create weak replies or pop-ups for the next shot. Som
items.author: Ana

items.title: Should You Target Women in Pickleball? The Honest Rec-Player Etiquette Guide
items.summary: Targeting a weaker player in pickleball is legal strategy, but targeting someone simply because she is a woman is lazy and often rude. In competitive play, target skill gaps. In social rec play, keep the game respectful, safe, and fun: play the player, not the stereotype. There a
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Swerve Serve in Pickleball: Smart Weapon or Consistency Trap?
items.summary: A pickleball swerve serve uses sidespin to make the ball bend or kick sideways, often pulling the returner off balance. It can be legal if the spin comes from paddle contact, not the release, but intermediate players should use it as a changeup—not a replacement for a reliable de
items.author: Ana

items.title: MLP Dallas Event Recap
items.summary: The first event of the 2026 Major League Pickleball (MLP) season was played this past weekend in Dallas at Pickler Universe and the new format and lineup changes were a huge hit. The LA Mad Drops left Dallas as event Champions.
items.author: Erik Tice

items.title: Why Right-Side Pickleball Is Harder Than Playing the Left
items.summary: Right side pickleball isn't just a different position—it's practically a different sport. The geometry, attack angles, and defensive responsibilities create challenges that left-side players simply don't face.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: PA Man Admits to Cutting Pickleball Nets After On-Court Injury 'Ruined' His Summer
items.summary: Pickleball court vandalism has taken a troubling turn in Monroe County, Pennsylvania, where a man admitted to cutting nets at two public parks after suffering a season-ending injury.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How Gabe Tardio's Paddle Grip Technique Is Changing Pro Pickleball
items.summary: Gabe Tardio's paddle grip technique of choking up on the paddle is revolutionizing how pro pickleball players approach hand speed and spin. The method, which involves positioning your index finger near the throat of the paddle, is spreading across the professional circuit.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Only Pickleball Serve You Need in 2026
items.summary: The pickleball serve has evolved dramatically over the past decade, and 2026 demands a modern approach that combines power, consistency, and strategy. Learn the exact technique, grip, and drills you need to master the only pickleball serve that matters.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Beginner Pickleball Mistakes That Are Costing You Points (And Easy Fixes)
items.summary: Most beginner pickleball mistakes come down to a handful of repeatable patterns, bad positioning, the wrong shot at the wrong time, and unforced errors you can fix this week. This guide breaks down the most common ones and gives you practical corrections to start winning more poi
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Develop Third Shot Drop Consistency: The Drill Protocol That Works
items.summary: If you want to improve third shot drop consistency, the answer isn't more court time, it's structured, intentional repetition. This guide breaks down the exact drill protocol that builds a reliable drop you can trust under pressure.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Why Pro Players Choke Up on Their Pickleball Grip
items.summary: Pro pickleball players aren't just gripping their paddles differently for show. Choking up on your pickleball grip lowers swing weight, increases hand speed, and improves control at the kitchen. Here's what you need to know.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Who Covers the Middle Ball at the Pickleball Kitchen? The Definitive Answer
items.summary: Knowing who covers middle kitchen pickleball is one of the most decisive tactical questions in doubles. This guide breaks down the rules of thumb, situational exceptions, and how the best players settle it automatically, no guesswork required.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Clear Skies Hospitality Acquires The Exchange, New Orleans’ Dedicated Pickleball Hub [VIDEO]
items.summary: New Orleans pickleball just got a bigger connection to one of Chicago’s fastest-growing pickleball lifestyle brands. Clear Skies Hospitality, the company behind Chicago’s SPF pickleball clubs, has acquired The Exchange, New Orleans’ first and only dedicated pickleball facility. T
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Overlooked Serve Tip That Adds Power Without Swinging Harder
items.summary: To add power to your pickleball serve, stop using only your arm. Start slightly closed, turn your hips and shoulders together, load the back leg, then transfer your weight forward through contact. This creates deeper, stronger serves with less effort and better control. A lot of
items.author: Ana

items.title: Should You Serve, Receive, or Choose Side?
items.summary: If you win the pickleball coin toss, don’t automatically choose serve. Pick side first if sun, wind, glare, or distractions matter. If conditions are neutral, receiving is often smart in doubles because your team can return deep, get forward, and settle into the match quickly. Mo
items.author: Ana

items.title: What to Eat Before Morning Pickleball Games
items.summary: Before morning pickleball, eat easy carbs with a little protein: banana with yogurt, toast with honey, oatmeal, cereal, or a small bagel. Eat a fuller meal 2–3 hours before, or a light carb snack 30–60 minutes before. Add water and electrolytes for long, hot session Morning pickl
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Biggest Block Volley Mistake in Pickleball (And Why It Kills Your Resets)
items.summary: A good block volley starts with a slightly open paddle face. If the face is too vertical or closed, the ball often drops into the net. Open it just enough to absorb pace, clear the net, and land softly in the kitchen. A lot of intermediate pickleball players think they have slow
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Forehand Crosscourt Roll That Gives Your Soft Game Teeth
items.summary: A forehand crosscourt roll is a topspin pressure shot that makes your dink game more dangerous. Hit it from balance, contact out front, stay low, brush across and up, and aim crosscourt to move opponents wide, force pop-ups, and create attacking chances without overhitting. The f
items.author: Ana

items.title: Should Better Players Be Expected to Teach During Open Play?
items.summary: Better players are not obligated to teach during regular pickleball open play. They should be respectful, safe, and helpful when asked, but beginners should not expect free coaching. The best rule: ask before giving advice, offer one quick tip if invited, and keep the game moving
items.author: Ana

items.title: Be a Tiger, Not a Cheetah: The Art of Speed Control
items.summary: One of the earliest lessons most pickleball players learn is simple: Get to the non-volley zone as fast as you can. While that advice isn’t wrong, it’s incomplete—and for many players it becomes a ceiling rather than a foundation. Winning points at higher levels isn’t about how f
items.author: Suzee Anderson

items.title: The Pickleball Puzzle: How to Outsmart, Outlast, and Outplay Every Opponent
items.summary: In pickleball, every game is a puzzle. Each point is another clue. The best players don’t just react, they solve. Whether you’re facing bangers or defensive grinders, your job is not simply to hit good shots. Your job is to decode and execute your plan against the equation in fro
items.author: Kyle McMakin

items.title: Back to Baseline: A Winning One-Day Meal Plan
items.summary: You may have watched the after-match interview of an adorable young pro player who openly admitted to having an “awful” diet. It caused chuckles across our pickle-munity to hear that this top athlete is flourishing despite his not-so-golden health habit. I think we all appreciate
items.author: Brandi Givens

items.title: LevelUp Pickleball Club Opens in Pittsburgh
items.summary: Levelup Pickleball Club Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania In the south suburbs of Pittsburgh, where football has normally dominated the sports landscape, pickleball has surged in popularity. Where there were once just about 200 avid pickleballers (eight years ago), that number is now clos
items.author: Drew Wathey

items.title: Rachel Rohrabacher
items.summary: Hometown: Tampa, FL Age: 28 Rating: 6.13 Favorite Event: PPA Cincinnati You were an outstanding doubles player in tennis at the University of South Carolina. What of those skills carried over into pickleball? Anticipation at the net and reaction time translated immediately, along
items.author: Alice Tym

items.title: Caribbean Capital of Pickleball
items.summary: With 71 courts across eight islands, Sandals and Beaches Resorts are rapidly becoming some of the world’s most exciting destinations for pickleball enthusiasts. Sandals South Coast In the world of luxury travel, the Caribbean has long been known as the capital of sun and sand, bu
items.author: Julie Talerico

items.title: Pickleball is the Destination
items.summary: Aurora Anguilla Resort These luxury getaways prove pickleball can be as refined as it is fun. As pickleball continues its global rise, select resorts are elevating the sport from a casual amenity to a defining pillar of the guest experience. In each issue of Pickleball Magazine,
items.author: Laura Gainor

items.title: Why Beginners Should Take Lessons
items.summary: When I was 5 years old, my mother took me for my first swim lesson. The instructor, a gruff man in red lifeguard Speedos, led me to the deep end of the pool and pushed me in. With my mother looking on in shock, I managed to dog-paddle to the side safely. At which point, the instr
items.author: Rick Cipes

items.title: SINGLES STYLES AND HOW TO PLAY AGAINST THEM
items.summary: You are better off going into a match with a plan. You need a baseline to be able to change a plan if it is not working. Good players will adjust, so you must be able to adjust as well. You certainly need to know yourself, what you can and cannot do. Good players have a weapon, a
items.author: Alice Tym

items.title: Wild Dunes Resort Pickleball Giveaway official rules
items.summary: OFFICIAL RULES Pickleball Magazine x Wild Dunes Resort x Pickleball in the Sun Sweepstakes NO PURCHASE NECESSARY TO ENTER OR WIN. A PURCHASE OR PAYMENT OF ANY KIND WILL NOT INCREASE YOUR CHANCES OF WINNING. VOID WHERE PROHIBITED OR RESTRICTED BY LAW. 1. Sponsor & Administrator Th
items.author: Laura Gainor

items.title: WHY SPORTSMANSHIP MATTERS: THE OBVIOUS AND NOT-SO-OBVIOUS REASONS
items.summary: We all want our sport to be respected. We don’t want to bring shame to pickleball as it develops into a recognized paddle sport. To honor the founding principles of the game and positively guide this development, we must work to exhibit good sportsmanship at all levels. Not only
items.author: Alice Tym

items.title: 7 Core Shots Every 4.0+ Player Must Master
items.summary: Elevating your game beyond intermediate isn’t about flashy winners—it’s about mastering essential concepts that improve consistency, control, and point construction. These seven fundamental shots separate a 3.5 from a dependable 4.0+ player, and practicing them with purpose makes
items.author: Kyle McMakin

items.title: Is Your Game One of Substance, or is it Reactionary?
items.summary: Becoming a better player involves the ability to dictate play. Most pickleball players begin by being reactionary: “Just get the return of serve back into the court.” “Keep the ball in play.” “Just get the ball in to start play—the serve doesn’t matter.” If your play is in line w
items.author: Alice Tym

items.title: Making Pickleball Fun for Kids!
items.summary: So what’s the secret for ensuring that your children or grandchildren have a positive first-time experience with pickleball, encouraging them to make it a lifetime activity they can love as much as we all do? First, remember your experience learning the game. Was it a friend or f
items.author: Jason Jamison

items.title: VARIATIONS ON THE BACKHAND SERVE
items.summary: As pickleball attracts more and more players from other sports, those new players bring shots from their former sports. There is much greater shot variety in pickleball than ever before. Some of the shots even have names, like reset, Erne, and dink. As overall play gets better, t
items.author: Alice Tym
